steps.title: Read values from table and compute difference
steps.content:
$$\text{Warehouse stores average} = 482,\quad \text{Supermarkets average} = 247$$
$$\text{Difference} = 482 - 247 = 235$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. The table shows that for a certain region in $2016$, the average number of store employees in warehouse stores was $365$ and the average number of store employees in supermarkets was $130$. Subtracting $130$ from $365$ yields $365-130$, or $235$. Therefore, the average number of store employees was $235$ greater in warehouse stores than in supermarkets.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. For this region in $2016$, this is how much greater the average number of store employees was in department stores than in supermarkets.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. For this region in $2016$, this is how much greater the average number of store employees was in warehouse stores than in department stores.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. For this region in $2016$, this is the sum of the average number of store employees in warehouse stores and in supermarkets.
